name: Master Taro
role: Flesh-Temple master confronting a threat the body can't meet
voiceHints: Calm, grounded, speaks slowly and moves precisely; a lifetime of breath-control in every sentence. Humble about the body, newly and uncomfortably aware of what the Temple never taught him. Asks for help like it's a discipline he's bad at.
knowledge: Soma at mastery — the body as instrument
That the Temple has a blind spot where the mind should be, and it's becoming dangerous
The health-and-healing arts of the body-pole
wants: To keep the Temple's devotion while opening it to what it refuses to learn — before the blind spot costs a life.
fears: That the thing that makes the Temple beautiful is the thing that will destroy it, and he can't train his way out of that.

reactsToReputation.thoughtful: seeks them as the mind the Temple lacks

companyNote: SNG-126 reference: a Flesh-Temple master travels as a TRAINER (his presence lets you reach the Somatics' capstones) and a LIAISON for his people. Aevi authors teaches/liaisonFor/recruitable across the wider roster.
appearance: Sixty, small and astonishingly dense, shaven head, skin like tanned hide over corded muscle. Both ears thickened from decades of grappling. Moves without hurry and never seems to shift his weight.
domainsNote: Derived from this NPC's authored spectrum against the great circle (SNG-174). Kind and domain are independent: a people tends toward positions, an individual holds their own.

curriculum.method: Demonstrates the capstone in the first hour — once, perfectly, at full speed. Then never again. You spend the next several years understanding what you already saw.
curriculum.opens: the highest rank, as a demonstration you cannot copy
curriculum.withholds: nothing. He believes withholding is a failure of nerve

curriculumNote: SNG-175 §3. DEVIATIONS ONLY — the curriculum spine derives from ability levelReq (285/285 carry it), so a teacher authors nothing but where they depart from the standard ladder. The departure IS the characterisation: two teachers of one tradition differ exactly here.
